display.purpose: Static-page heroes and rare large product moments.

headline.purpose: Page titles, record titles, and major section names.

body.purpose: Default product UI, forms, lists, and dense data.

label.purpose: Uppercase section labels, tags, metadata, and stamp-like status.

shadows.name: raised-surface
shadows.purpose: Hovered cards, dropdowns, and focused panels.

shadows.name: floating-surface
shadows.purpose: Modals, combo dropdowns, and high-priority overlays.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Default button, input, tab, and dropdown state changes.

motion.name: entrance-soft
motion.purpose: Feed card entrances and light page reveals.

motion.name: craft-expo
motion.purpose: Onboarding and crafted static-page entrance moments.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main action button for login, save, and primary page actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; background: var(--btn-primary-bg); color: var(--btn-primary-text); font-family: 'Iosevka Patrick', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--btn-primary-bg-hover); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--input-border-focus); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A bordered lower-priority action with warm surface treatment.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid var(--btn-secondary-border); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--btn-secondary-bg); color: var(--btn-secondary-text); font-family: 'Iosevka Patrick', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--btn-secondary-bg-hover); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--input-border-focus); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Form Input
components.description: The default text field vocabulary for forms and modals.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 8px; color: var(--text-primary); font-family: 'Iosevka Patrick', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-input { min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--input-border); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--input-bg); color: var(--text-primary); font: inherit; font-size: 16px; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease, background-color 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--text-placeholder); } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--input-border-focus); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--input-ring-focus); background: var(--input-bg-focus); outline: none; }

components.name: Card
components.description: A warm bordered product container with modest lift.
components.html:
Track Your Brews

Log beans, grind, water, time, and tasting notes.
components.css: .ds-card { padding: 24px; border: 1px solid var(--card-border); border-radius: 12px; background: var(--card-bg); color: var(--text-primary); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); font-family: 'Iosevka Patrick', ui-monospace, monospace; transition: box-shadow 200ms ease, transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 20px; line-height: 1.4; font-weight: 600; } .ds-card p { margin: 0; color: var(--text-secondary); line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Filter Pill
components.description: A compact entity or filter selector with quiet inactive state and strong active state.
components.css: .ds-filter-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin-right: 8px; padding: 4px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--card-border); border-radius: 9999px; background: transparent; color: var(--text-muted); font-family: 'Iosevka Patrick', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-filter-pill:hover { color: var(--text-secondary); border-color: var(--input-border-focus); background: var(--surface-bg); } .ds-filter-pill-active { color: var(--btn-primary-text); background: var(--btn-primary-bg); border-color: var(--btn-primary-bg); } .ds-filter-pill:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--input-border-focus); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Feed Note
components.description: A sticky-note style feed card on the community corkboard.
components.html:
Brew

Morning V60

Bright, peachy, dialed a touch finer.
components.css: .ds-feed-note { max-width: 280px; padding: 16px; border: 0; border-radius: 4px; background-color: var(--feed-card-bg); background-image: linear-gradient(var(--type-brew-tint), var(--type-brew-tint)); color: var(--text-primary); box-shadow: 1px 2px 4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0 1px 2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.06); rotate: -1deg; font-family: 'Iosevka Patrick', ui-monospace, monospace; transition: box-shadow 200ms ease, transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), rotate 200ms ease; } .ds-feed-note:hover { rotate: 0deg; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); } .ds-type { display: inline-flex; padding: 2px 6px; border: 1px solid currentColor; border-radius: 4px; color: var(--type-brew); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-feed-note h3 { margin: 12px 0 6px; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-feed-note p { margin: 0; color: var(--text-secondary); line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Combo Dropdown
components.description: The entity picker dropdown used for beans, roasters, brewers, grinders, and related records.
components.html:
Your beans
Ethiopia GujiPassenger Coffee
Create new bean
components.css: .ds-combo { width: 320px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid var(--input-border); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--card-bg); color: var(--text-primary); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); font-family: 'Iosevka Patrick', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-combo-label { padding: 6px 12px; background: var(--surface-bg); color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-combo-item, .ds-combo-create { padding: 8px 12px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; } .ds-combo-item:hover, .ds-combo-create:hover { background: var(--surface-bg); } .ds-combo-item span { display: block; color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 12px; } .ds-combo-create { border-top: 1px solid var(--surface-border); color: var(--text-muted); font-weight: 500; }

narrative.overview:
Arabica should feel like a well-used coffee notebook left on the counter at a neighborhood cafe. It is social, but not performative. It is technical, but never cold. The interface supports the ritual of brewing: slow enough to feel intentional, structured enough to keep data useful, warm enough to invite return visits.

The visual system blends community cafe atmosphere with analog lab notes. Product surfaces use familiar app patterns so people can log a brew, edit a bean, or scan a feed without learning invented controls. Craft details carry the personality: kraft texture, corkboard feed surfaces, sticky-note cards, entity-tinted washes, dashed rubber-stamp progress, and coffee-toned neutrals.

Arabica explicitly rejects generic SaaS dashboards, corporate productivity tools, overly techy developer aesthetics, and social media feed clones. Data should feel warm, not cold. The feed should feel like overhearing conversations at a cafe, not scrolling a timeline.
keyCharacteristics: Cozy, social, inviting, and grounded in coffee ritual.
Familiar product UI with craft details, not novelty controls.
Warm brown and cream neutrals with restrained amber and entity accents.
Tactile texture and modest lift, not glass, neon, or glossy polish.
Light and dark themes both supported, with the same coffee-shop warmth.

rules.name: The Brown Paper Rule
rules.body: Tint every neutral toward coffee. New surfaces should start from Paper Cream, Sticky Paper, Paper Blush, or Espresso Ink, never from sterile gray.

rules.name: The Accent Has a Job Rule
rules.body: Amber is for attention and state. Entity colors are for entity identity. Do not use saturated color as decoration without a semantic role.

rules.name: The Corkboard Rule
rules.body: Social feed surfaces may be more tactile than utility pages. Cork texture and sticky-note treatment belong in community context, not every settings panel.

rules.name: The Notebook Mono Rule
rules.body: Iosevka Patrick is the default voice. Do not introduce a second typeface unless a page genuinely needs a softer display voice and the choice is documented.

rules.name: The Label Restraint Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracking is for labels and tags, not whole paragraphs or button-heavy regions.

rules.name: The Border First Rule
rules.body: Use a warm 1px border before reaching for a large shadow. The interface should feel printed and tactile, not floating in space.

rules.name: The Lift on Intent Rule
rules.body: A 2px hover lift is enough. Elevation should confirm interaction, not perform choreography.

dos: Do use Paper Cream as the environmental base and Espresso Ink as the main anchor.
Do keep product flows familiar: standard buttons, inputs, dropdowns, tabs, and modals with predictable states.
Do use entity colors for entity identity: bean, brew, brewer, grinder, roaster, recipe, tea, vendor, cafe, and drink.
Do add texture where it supports the metaphor: kraft containers, cork feed boards, and sticky notes.
Do keep motion short and state-based: 100 to 250ms for most UI, 300 to 380ms only for page or feed entrances.
Do write empty states that name the next action, such as "add the bag you're on now".
Do preserve dark mode warmth. Dark surfaces should be coffee-black and brown, not neutral charcoal.
donts: Don't make Arabica look like generic SaaS dashboards or corporate productivity tools.
Don't use an overly techy/developer aesthetic. Data should feel warm, not cold.
Don't make the feed feel like Instagram, Twitter, or any social media feed clone with no personality.
Don't use colored `border-left` or `border-right` accents greater than 1px on cards, callouts, alerts, or list items.
Don't use gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, neon-on-black, or hero-metric SaaS templates.
Don't wrap everything in identical card grids. Use cards when they clarify grouping, not as the default answer.
Don't add new pure black or pure white neutrals. Existing legacy white card tokens should not be the model for new surfaces.
Don't use emoji as primary UI glyphs. Use inline SVG with `stroke="currentColor"`.
Don't animate layout properties or use bounce/elastic easings. Motion should feel like a hand placing a note, not an app trying to impress.
